description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: economic programs of U.S. president Franklin D. Roosevelt
description_wiki: The New Deal was a 1933–1938 series of economic, social, and political reforms in response to the Great Depression in the United States under President Franklin D. Roosevelt.

description: president of the United States from 1945 to 1953; politician (1884–1972)
description_wiki: Harry S. Truman  (May 8, 1884 – December 26, 1972) was the 33rd president of the United States, serving from 1945 to 1953.

description: upper house of the United States Congress
description_wiki: The United States Senate is a chamber of the bicameral United States Congress; it is the upper house, and the U.S. House of Representatives is the lower house.

description: phenomenon in the United States of making accusations of subversion or treason without proper regard for evidence, named after Senator Joseph McCarthy
description_wiki: McCarthyism is a political practice defined by the political repression and persecution of left-wing individuals and a campaign spreading fear of communist and Soviet influence on American institutions and of Soviet espionage in the United States during the late 1940s through the 1950s, heavily associated with the Second Red Scare, also known as the McCarthy era.

aliases: Republican Party of the United States
description: American political party
description_wiki: The Republican Party, commonly known as the Grand Old Party (GOP), is the major conservative and right-wing political party in the United States.

description: president of the United States from 1929 to 1933 (1874–1964)
description_wiki: Herbert Clark Hoover (August 10, 1874 – October 20, 1964) was the 31st president of the United States, serving from 1929 to 1933.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Alabama ( , AL-ə-BAM-ə) is a state in the Southeastern and Deep Southern regions of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki:
Florida (  FLORR-ih-də; Spanish: [floˈɾiða] ) is a state in the Southeastern and South Atlantic
regions of the United States.

description: family name
description_wiki: Hill is a surname of English and Scottish origin, meaning "a person who lived on a hill".

description: American jurist and politician (1899–1985)
description_wiki: John Jackson Sparkman (December 20, 1899 – November 16, 1985) was an American jurist and politician from the state of Alabama.

description: American politician (1909–1991)
family name
description_wiki: Gurney is an English surname of Anglo-Norman origin.

description: American politician (1930–1998)
description_wiki: Lawton Mainor Chiles Jr.

description: American politician (1928-2014)
description_wiki: Reubin O'Donovan Askew (September 11, 1928 – March 13, 2014) was an American politician, who served as the 37th governor of Florida from 1971 to 1979.

description: public university located in Tuscaloosa, Alabama, United States
description_wiki: The University of Alabama (informally known as Alabama, UA, the Capstone, or Bama) is a public research university in Tuscaloosa, Alabama, United States.

description: town in Orange County, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Chapel Hill is a town in the counties of Orange and Durham, North Carolina, United States.

description: law school of Harvard University in Cambridge, Massachusetts
description_wiki: Harvard Law School (HLS) is the law school of Harvard University, a private Ivy League research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts.

description: public research university in Fayetteville, Arkansas, USA
description_wiki: The University of Arkansas (U of A, UArk, or UA) is a public land-grant research university in Fayetteville, Arkansas, United States.

description: city in Taylor County, Florida, United States
description_wiki: Perry is a city and the county seat of Taylor County, Florida, United States.

description: county seat of Leon County, Florida, USA and Florida federated state capital city
description_wiki: Tallahassee ( TAL-ə-HASS-ee) is the capital city of the U.S. state of Florida.

description: Period from 1932-1960 when the Democratic Party dominated American politics
description_wiki: The New Deal coalition was an American political coalition that supported the Democratic Party beginning in 1932.

description: public university in Auburn, Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Auburn University (informally known as AU or Auburn) is a public land-grant research university in Auburn, Alabama, United States.

description: railroad constructed to join Macon, Georgia, and Savannah, Georgia
description_wiki: The Central of Georgia Railway (reporting mark CG; abbreviated CofG) was a railroad in Georgia, United States, that was active from 1895–1963.

aliases: Democratic Party of the United States
description: American political party
description_wiki: The Democratic Party is the major liberal political party in the United States, sitting on the center to center-left of the political spectrum.
